queries.name: Start here: Iceberg Catalog Explorer install
queries.description: Read me first. The walkthrough for installing ice_meta_<catalog> navigation views with the generator entries below.

cfg.content:
## Iceberg Catalog Explorer — install walkthrough

Turns the raw Iceberg metadata of a catalog into queryable ClickHouse views
(`ice_meta_<catalog>.tables / snapshots / schema_columns / partition_spec /
manifest_lists / objects / metadata_log` + parameterized `data_files(prefix)` /
`parquet_stats(prefix)`), plus a cross-catalog union layer `ice_meta_all`.

**Precondition**: the catalog must be a REST catalog whose warehouse is a plain
`s3://` bucket the ClickHouse pod's IAM role can read (keyless `s3()`).
Glue vended-credentials and S3Tables (ARN warehouse) catalogs are **not**
supported by this raw-S3 approach. Also make sure
`access_control_improvements.enable_read_write_grants = true` on the server,
or the bucket-scoped S3 grant used by the views fail-opens to unrestricted S3.

**Steps** (run 1–3 on a connection that may CREATE databases/views/users/roles
and hold+grant `READ ON S3`):

1. **Preflight** — fill `warehouse_url` (https form) on the *Preflight* entry
   and Run. It must report your tables; an access error or 0 files means stop.
2. **Install the catalog** — fill `catalog` (short name, e.g. `ice`),
   `warehouse_url`, `glob_depth` (`2` for the usual `namespace/table` layout)
   and `grant_role` (an existing role/user to grant read access to, or `none`)
   on *Generate: catalog install DDL*. Run, **copy the raw result**, paste into
   a new tab, Run. Repeat per catalog.
3. **Union layer** — Run *Generate: ice_meta_all union DDL* (no inputs), copy
   the result into a new tab, Run. Re-run this step whenever you add or remove
   a catalog.
4. *(optional, needs the chmem memory store + `memory_writer`)* — *Generate:
   chmem seed facts INSERT*: run, copy, run in a new tab. Makes the views
   self-describing to MCP agents.
5. **Drill-down tiles** — *Generate: per-catalog drill-down Library*: run,
   copy the JSON, save as `iceberg-<catalog>-drilldown.json`, then
   File ▸ Append it. Gives per-table small-file / partition-skew / Parquet
   tiles for that catalog.

Then File ▸ Append `iceberg-catalog-dashboard.json` (BI) and/or
`iceberg-dba-dashboard.json` (DBA + log panels) and open the Dashboard.

To **remove** a catalog, see the comment block at the end of its generated
install script.

queries.name: Preflight: is this catalog ice_meta-compatible?
queries.sql:
WITH throwIf(NOT startsWith({warehouse_url:String}, 'https://'), 'warehouse_url must be the https:// virtual-host form of the bucket, e.g. https://my-bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com (convert s3://my-bucket)') AS _g
SELECT
  count()                                                    AS metadata_json_files,
  uniqExact(extract(_path, '([^/]+/[^/]+)/metadata/'))       AS tables_found,
  if(count() = 0,
     'NOT COMPATIBLE: bucket is readable but holds no <namespace>/<table>/metadata/*.metadata.json objects at depth 2. Wrong warehouse_url, non-standard layout (try a different glob_depth in the install generator), or not an Iceberg REST warehouse.',
     'OK: warehouse is raw-S3 readable and has Iceberg tables at depth 2 (namespace/table). Proceed with "Generate: catalog install DDL".') AS verdict
FROM s3(concat(trim(TRAILING '/' FROM {warehouse_url:String}), '/*/*/metadata/*.metadata.json'), 'RawBLOB')
queries.description: Step 1. Fill warehouse_url with the https:// form of the catalog warehouse bucket (s3://my-bucket -> https://my-bucket.s3.<region>.amazonaws.com) and Run ON THE INSTALLER/ADMIN CONNECTION. An S3 AccessDenied error = the ClickHouse server role cannot read that bucket (Glue vended-credentials catalogs fail here by design) - do not install. A "Host is empty in S3 URI" / URI-parse error = the value is not an https:// bucket URL (an S3Tables ARN warehouse, for example, is not supported). Checks the standard depth-2 namespace/table layout.

queries.name: Generate: catalog install DDL
queries.sql:
WITH
  throwIf(NOT match({catalog:String}, '^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$'), 'catalog must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ (it becomes a database/role name suffix)') AS _g1,
  throwIf(NOT startsWith({warehouse_url:String}, 'https://'), 'warehouse_url must be the https:// virtual-host form of the bucket, e.g. https://my-bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com (convert s3://my-bucket)') AS _g2,
  trim(TRAILING '/' FROM {warehouse_url:String}) AS _url,
  arrayStringConcat(arrayMap(x -> '*', range(toUInt64(greatest({glob_depth:UInt8}, 1)))), '/') AS _glob,
  arrayStringConcat(arrayMap(x -> '[^/]+', range(toUInt64(greatest({glob_depth:UInt8}, 1)))), '/') AS _re,
  if(lower({grant_role:String}) IN ('none', '-', ''), '-- (no reader-role grant requested; GRANT ice_meta_' || {catalog:String} || '_reader_role TO <role-or-user> yourself)', 'GRANT ice_meta_' || {catalog:String} || '_reader_role TO `' || {grant_role:String} || '`;') AS _grant_line
SELECT replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(replaceAll(
  '-- ============================================================================
-- ice_meta___CATALOG__: navigable views over the Iceberg metadata of catalog
-- ''__CATALOG__'' (warehouse __WAREHOUSE_URL__).
--
-- GENERATED by the "Generate: catalog install DDL" Library entry
-- (altinity-sql-browser examples/iceberg-install.json). Run the whole script
-- on an ADMIN connection (multiquery). Re-runnable / idempotent.
--
-- PRECONDITIONS (run the "Preflight" Library entry first):
--  * The catalog is a REST catalog whose warehouse is a plain s3:// bucket
--    readable by this ClickHouse server''s pod IAM role (keyless s3() access).
--    Glue vended-credentials and S3Tables (ARN warehouse) catalogs are NOT
--    supported by this raw-S3 approach.
--  * access_control_improvements.enable_read_write_grants should be enabled,
--    or the bucket-scoped `GRANT READ ON S3(...)` below silently degrades to
--    unrestricted S3 access for the definer (fail-open; see
--    https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse docs on read/write grants).
--
-- Design (same as the original antalya ice_meta):
--  * Views are the ONLY interface. They run as a dedicated least-privilege
--    definer user `s3_reader` (DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER), so
--    readers need only SELECT on the view — NOT the S3 source privilege.
--  * s3_reader is shared across catalogs: one user, one bucket-scoped READ
--    grant per catalog warehouse. Removal commands are at the end of this file.
--  * Old-analyzer compatible: no QUALIFY, no two-level ARRAY JOIN —
--    row_number() + arrayFilter(...)[1] on purpose. Do not "modernize".
-- ============================================================================

CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS ice_meta___CATALOG__ ENGINE = Atomic;

-- Least-privilege definer for every view. HOST NONE => cannot authenticate;
-- it only ever exists to lend its (minimal) privileges to the views'' bodies.
CREATE USER IF NOT EXISTS s3_reader NOT IDENTIFIED HOST NONE;
GRANT CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE ON *.* TO s3_reader;                 -- s3() prerequisite
GRANT READ ON S3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/.*'') TO s3_reader;
-- Lets the ice_meta_all union layer (also DEFINER = s3_reader) read through:
GRANT SELECT ON ice_meta___CATALOG__.* TO s3_reader;

-- One row per Iceberg table (from the current metadata.json).
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.tables
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
WITH files AS (
  SELECT s3_prefix, metadata_file, j FROM (
    SELECT extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'') AS s3_prefix,
           _file AS metadata_file,
           raw_blob AS j,
           row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'')
                              ORDER BY toUInt64OrZero(substring(_file,1,position(_file,''-'')-1)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/*.metadata.json'',''RawBLOB'')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'')                             AS ice_table,
  s3_prefix,
  metadata_file                                             AS current_metadata_file,
  JSONExtractInt(j,''format-version'')                        AS format_version,
  JSONExtractString(j,''table-uuid'')                         AS table_uuid,
  JSONExtractString(j,''location'')                           AS location,
  JSONExtractString(j,''current-snapshot-id'')                AS current_snapshot_id,
  fromUnixTimestamp64Milli(JSONExtractInt(j,''last-updated-ms'')) AS last_updated,
  JSONExtractInt(j,''last-column-id'')                        AS last_column_id,
  length(JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''snapshots''))                AS num_snapshots,
  length(JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''schemas''))                  AS num_schemas,
  JSONExtractRaw(j,''properties'')                            AS properties
FROM files
ORDER BY ice_table;

-- One row per (table, snapshot) from the current metadata''s snapshot log.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.snapshots
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
WITH files AS (
  SELECT s3_prefix, j FROM (
    SELECT extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'') AS s3_prefix,
           raw_blob AS j,
           row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'')
                              ORDER BY toUInt64OrZero(substring(_file,1,position(_file,''-'')-1)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/*.metadata.json'',''RawBLOB'')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'')                              AS ice_table,
  JSONExtractString(s,''snapshot-id'')                         AS snapshot_id,
  JSONExtractString(s,''parent-snapshot-id'')                  AS parent_snapshot_id,
  JSONExtractInt(s,''sequence-number'')                        AS sequence_number,
  fromUnixTimestamp64Milli(JSONExtractInt(s,''timestamp-ms'')) AS committed_at,
  JSONExtractString(s,''summary'',''operation'')                 AS operation,
  JSONExtractInt(s,''summary'',''added-records'')                AS added_records,
  JSONExtractInt(s,''summary'',''total-records'')                AS total_records,
  JSONExtractInt(s,''summary'',''total-data-files'')             AS total_data_files,
  JSONExtractInt(s,''summary'',''total-files-size'')             AS total_files_size,
  JSONExtractRaw(s,''summary'')                                AS summary,
  JSONExtractString(s,''manifest-list'')                       AS manifest_list_path
FROM files
ARRAY JOIN JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''snapshots'') AS s
ORDER BY ice_table, sequence_number;

-- One row per column of each table''s current schema.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.schema_columns
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
WITH files AS (
  SELECT s3_prefix, j FROM (
    SELECT extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'') AS s3_prefix,
           raw_blob AS j,
           row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'')
                              ORDER BY toUInt64OrZero(substring(_file,1,position(_file,''-'')-1)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/*.metadata.json'',''RawBLOB'')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  ice_table,
  JSONExtractInt(cur_schema,''schema-id'') AS schema_id,
  JSONExtractInt(f,''id'')                 AS field_id,
  JSONExtractString(f,''name'')            AS name,
  JSONExtractRaw(f,''type'')               AS type,
  JSONExtractBool(f,''required'')          AS required
FROM (
  SELECT replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'') AS ice_table,
         arrayFilter(x -> JSONExtractInt(x,''schema-id'') = JSONExtractInt(j,''current-schema-id''),
                     JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''schemas''))[1] AS cur_schema
  FROM files
)
ARRAY JOIN JSONExtractArrayRaw(cur_schema,''fields'') AS f
ORDER BY ice_table, field_id;

-- One row per partition field of each table''s default partition spec.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.partition_spec
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
WITH files AS (
  SELECT s3_prefix, j FROM (
    SELECT extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'') AS s3_prefix,
           raw_blob AS j,
           row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'')
                              ORDER BY toUInt64OrZero(substring(_file,1,position(_file,''-'')-1)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/*.metadata.json'',''RawBLOB'')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  ice_table,
  JSONExtractInt(cur_spec,''spec-id'')   AS spec_id,
  JSONExtractInt(pf,''field-id'')        AS field_id,
  JSONExtractInt(pf,''source-id'')       AS source_id,
  JSONExtractString(pf,''name'')         AS name,
  JSONExtractString(pf,''transform'')    AS transform
FROM (
  SELECT replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'') AS ice_table,
         arrayFilter(x -> JSONExtractInt(x,''spec-id'') = JSONExtractInt(j,''default-spec-id''),
                     JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''partition-specs''))[1] AS cur_spec
  FROM files
)
ARRAY JOIN JSONExtractArrayRaw(cur_spec,''fields'') AS pf
ORDER BY ice_table, spec_id, field_id;

-- One row per manifest referenced by a manifest-list (snap-*.avro).
-- list_snapshot_id = snapshot that OWNS this list (from snap-<id>-... filename);
-- added_snapshot_id = snapshot that added the manifest. For a table''s live set:
--   WHERE list_snapshot_id = (SELECT current_snapshot_id FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.tables t WHERE t.ice_table = m.ice_table)
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.manifest_lists
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
SELECT
  replaceAll(extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/''),''/'',''.'') AS ice_table,
  extract(_file,''snap-([0-9]+)-'')                              AS list_snapshot_id,
  _file                       AS manifest_list_file,
  manifest_path,
  manifest_length,
  content,
  added_snapshot_id,
  sequence_number,
  added_files_count,
  existing_files_count,
  deleted_files_count,
  added_rows_count,
  existing_rows_count,
  deleted_rows_count,
  length(partitions)          AS partition_count
FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/snap-*.avro'',''Avro'')
ORDER BY ice_table, list_snapshot_id, sequence_number;

-- Every object in the warehouse bucket, bucketed by Iceberg role. Backs the
-- object-storage census and orphan detection without any raw s3() for the reader.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.objects
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
SELECT
  replaceAll(extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/(data|metadata)/''),''/'',''.'') AS ice_table,
  multiIf(endsWith(_file,''.metadata.json''),''metadata.json'',
          endsWith(_file,''.avro'') AND startsWith(_file,''snap-''),''manifest-list'',
          endsWith(_file,''.avro''),''manifest'',
          endsWith(_file,''.parquet''),''data.parquet'',''other'') AS kind,
  _file  AS file,
  _size  AS size_bytes,
  _path  AS path
FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/**'',''One'');

-- Per-data-file detail for ONE table. Parameterized: pass the slash-form prefix
-- (see ice_meta___CATALOG__.tables.s3_prefix), e.g.
--   SELECT * FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix = ''namespace/table'');
-- A single all-tables view is intentionally NOT provided: the manifest avro''s
-- data_file.partition tuple has a table-specific shape, so a cross-table glob
-- fails Avro schema binding. To span tables, UNION per-prefix calls in a query.
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files;
CREATE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
SELECT
  {prefix:String}                       AS s3_prefix,
  data_file.file_path AS file_path,
  data_file.file_format AS file_format,
  data_file.record_count AS record_count,
  data_file.file_size_in_bytes AS file_size,
  toString(data_file.partition) AS partition
FROM s3(concat(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/'', {prefix:String}, ''/metadata/*-m*.avro''),''Avro'')
ORDER BY file_path;

-- Prior metadata.json versions per table (the Iceberg `metadata-log`), one row each.
-- Powers a "metadata version timeline" — history/metadata-bloat signal.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.metadata_log
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
WITH files AS (
  SELECT s3_prefix, j FROM (
    SELECT extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'') AS s3_prefix,
           raw_blob AS j,
           row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY extract(_path,''(__NS_RE__)/metadata/'')
                              ORDER BY toUInt64OrZero(substring(_file,1,position(_file,''-'')-1)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM s3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/__NS_GLOB__/metadata/*.metadata.json'',''RawBLOB'')
  ) WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'')                             AS ice_table,
  fromUnixTimestamp64Milli(JSONExtractInt(e,''timestamp-ms'')) AS made_current_at,
  JSONExtractString(e,''metadata-file'')                      AS metadata_file
FROM files
ARRAY JOIN JSONExtractArrayRaw(j,''metadata-log'') AS e
ORDER BY ice_table, made_current_at;

-- Parquet internals per table (compression ratio, row groups). Parameterized:
--   SELECT * FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.parquet_stats(prefix = ''namespace/table'');
-- SLOW: reads every data-file footer (~4s / 40 files) — use on demand, not a live tile.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta___CATALOG__.parquet_stats
DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER
AS
SELECT
  {prefix:String}                                            AS s3_prefix,
  count()                                                    AS files,
  sum(num_row_groups)                                        AS row_groups,
  round(avg(num_row_groups),2)                               AS avg_rg_per_file,
  toUInt64(round(avg(num_rows)))                             AS avg_rows_per_file,
  round(sum(total_uncompressed_size)/sum(total_compressed_size),3) AS compression_ratio
FROM s3(concat(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/'', {prefix:String}, ''/data/**/*.parquet''),''ParquetMetadata'');

-- ============================================================================
-- Access. Views are the only interface — readers get SELECT on the catalog''s
-- ice_meta DB and nothing else (no raw S3 privilege; the s3_reader definer
-- supplies that inside the views).
-- ============================================================================
CREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS ice_meta___CATALOG___reader_role;
GRANT SHOW, SELECT ON ice_meta___CATALOG__.* TO ice_meta___CATALOG___reader_role;
__GRANT_ROLE_LINE__

-- ============================================================================
-- To REMOVE catalog ''__CATALOG__'' later:
--   REVOKE READ ON S3(''__WAREHOUSE_URL__/.*'') FROM s3_reader;
--   REVOKE SELECT ON ice_meta___CATALOG__.* FROM s3_reader;
--   DROP ROLE IF EXISTS ice_meta___CATALOG___reader_role;  -- revoke from grantees first
--   DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS ice_meta___CATALOG__;
-- then re-run the "Generate: ice_meta_all union DDL" Library entry.
-- ============================================================================
',
  '__GRANT_ROLE_LINE__', _grant_line),
  '__WAREHOUSE_URL__', _url),
  '__NS_GLOB__', _glob),
  '__NS_RE__', _re),
  '__CATALOG__', {catalog:String}) AS install_sql
FORMAT TSVRaw
queries.description: Step 2, once per catalog. Inputs: catalog = short lowercase name (becomes database ice_meta_<catalog>); warehouse_url = https:// bucket root from the preflight; glob_depth = 2 for the standard namespace/table layout (3 for an extra nesting level); grant_role = existing role or user to receive read access, or none. Run, then COPY the single raw result cell into a NEW TAB on an admin connection and Run it there (multiquery). Idempotent - safe to re-run.

queries.name: Generate: ice_meta_all union DDL
queries.sql:
WITH cats AS (
  SELECT name AS db, replaceOne(name, 'ice_meta_', '') AS catalog
  FROM system.databases
  WHERE match(name, '^ice_meta_[a-z0-9_]+$') AND name != 'ice_meta_all'
)
SELECT concat(
  '-- ice_meta_all: cross-catalog union layer, generated from the live set of\n',
  '-- ice_meta_<catalog> databases (', (SELECT arrayStringConcat(arraySort(groupArray(catalog)), ', ') FROM cats), ').\n',
  '-- Re-generate + re-run whenever a catalog is added or removed.\n\n',
  'CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS ice_meta_all ENGINE = Atomic;\n\n',
  arrayStringConcat(arraySort(groupArray(ddl)), '\n'),
  '\nCREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS ice_meta_all_reader_role;\n',
  'GRANT SHOW, SELECT ON ice_meta_all.* TO ice_meta_all_reader_role;\n') AS union_sql
FROM (
  SELECT v, concat(
    'CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ice_meta_all.', v, '\n',
    'DEFINER = s3_reader SQL SECURITY DEFINER\nAS\n',
    arrayStringConcat(arraySort(groupArray(
      concat('SELECT ''', catalog, ''' AS catalog, * FROM ', db, '.', v))), '\nUNION ALL\n'),
    ';\n') AS ddl
  FROM cats
  CROSS JOIN (SELECT arrayJoin(['tables','snapshots','schema_columns','partition_spec','manifest_lists','objects','metadata_log']) AS v) AS vs
  GROUP BY v
)
WHERE throwIf((SELECT count() FROM cats) = 0, 'no ice_meta_<catalog> databases found - run "Generate: catalog install DDL" first') = 0
FORMAT TSVRaw
queries.description: Step 3, and again after every catalog add/remove. No inputs - it discovers the installed ice_meta_<catalog> databases from system.databases and emits DDL for ice_meta_all: the 7 zero-arg views unioned across catalogs with a leading `catalog` column (data_files/parquet_stats stay per-catalog - Avro schema binding is table-specific). Copy the raw result into a new tab on the admin connection and Run.

queries.name: Generate: chmem seed facts INSERT
queries.sql:
WITH
  throwIf(NOT match({catalog:String}, '^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$'), 'catalog must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ (it becomes a database/role name suffix)') AS _g1
SELECT concat(
  '-- chmem seed for ice_meta_', {catalog:String}, ' - run as a user holding memory_writer.\n',
  '-- async_insert must be 0 or the store records an empty author (currentUser() blanks).\n',
  'INSERT INTO mcp.entries_raw (source_db, facts) SETTINGS async_insert = 0 VALUES (''ice_meta_', {catalog:String}, ''', ''',
  replaceAll(replaceAll('[
  {
    "kind": "overview",
    "slug": "db-overview",
    "title": "ice_meta___CATALOG__ = navigation views over Iceberg catalog ''__CATALOG__'' metadata",
    "body": "Read-only views over the raw Iceberg metadata of catalog `__CATALOG__` (NOT the table data itself — query the `__CATALOG__` database for data). Zero-arg views: `tables` (one row per Iceberg table, current metadata), `snapshots` (full snapshot history), `schema_columns` (current schema), `partition_spec` (default spec), `manifest_lists` (manifest inventory), `objects` (whole-warehouse object census), `metadata_log` (prior metadata.json versions). Parameterized per-table views: `data_files(prefix)`, `parquet_stats(prefix)` — pass `tables.s3_prefix` (slash form `namespace/table`). Cross-catalog: same views with a leading `catalog` column live in `ice_meta_all`.",
    "tables": [],
    "tags": ["iceberg", "metadata", "catalog", "overview", "s3"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "note",
    "slug": "tables-naming-convention",
    "title": "ice_table is dot-form; s3_prefix is the slash-form key for parameterized views",
    "body": "`ice_table = replaceAll(s3_prefix,''/'',''.'')` (e.g. `logs/pod_logs` -> `logs.pod_logs`). The parameterized views `data_files(prefix)` / `parquet_stats(prefix)` require the raw slash form from `tables.s3_prefix`, not the dot form.",
    "tables": ["tables"],
    "tags": ["naming", "prefix"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "note",
    "slug": "snapshots-need-argmax-for-current",
    "title": "snapshots is full history — current totals need argMax by sequence_number",
    "body": "`snapshots` has one row per (table, snapshot) over all history. For CURRENT per-table totals use `argMax(total_records, sequence_number)` / `argMax(total_files_size, sequence_number)` grouped by `ice_table`, or join `tables.current_snapshot_id = snapshots.snapshot_id`.",
    "tables": ["snapshots"],
    "tags": ["aggregation", "current-state"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "note",
    "slug": "data-files-no-cross-table-view",
    "title": "No all-tables data_files view — Avro partition tuple is table-specific",
    "body": "A cross-table glob over manifest `.avro` files fails Avro schema binding because `data_file.partition` has a per-table shape. To span tables, UNION per-prefix calls: `SELECT * FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix=''ns/t1'') UNION ALL SELECT * FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix=''ns/t2'')`. Get prefixes from `tables.s3_prefix`.",
    "tables": ["tables"],
    "tags": ["limitation", "avro", "union"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "note",
    "slug": "parquet-stats-is-heavy",
    "title": "parquet_stats(prefix) reads every data-file footer — on demand only",
    "body": "`parquet_stats` opens every `.parquet` footer under the table''s data/ path (~4s per 40 files). Use for on-demand tuning checks, never as a live dashboard tile.",
    "tables": ["tables"],
    "tags": ["performance", "cost"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "note",
    "slug": "objects-view-is-full-bucket-census",
    "title": "objects lists the ENTIRE warehouse bucket, classified by Iceberg role",
    "body": "`objects` globs the whole warehouse (`**`) and classifies each object as metadata.json / manifest-list / manifest / data.parquet / other by filename. Use it against `snapshots`/`data_files` for orphan-file detection; it is not a per-table filter primitive by itself (one full listing per query).",
    "tables": ["objects"],
    "tags": ["census", "orphans", "cost"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "recipe",
    "slug": "rows-per-table",
    "title": "Current row count per Iceberg table (metadata only, cheap)",
    "body": "Reads only metadata.json snapshots — no data scan.",
    "sql": "SELECT ice_table, argMax(total_records, sequence_number) AS rows FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.snapshots GROUP BY ice_table ORDER BY rows DESC",
    "tables": ["snapshots"],
    "tags": ["size", "rows"],
    "cost_hint": "cheap",
    "verified": true
  },
  {
    "kind": "recipe",
    "slug": "storage-per-table-gib",
    "title": "Current storage footprint per table in GiB (metadata only, cheap)",
    "body": "Current total_files_size per table from the latest snapshot.",
    "sql": "SELECT ice_table, round(argMax(total_files_size, sequence_number) / 1024 / 1024 / 1024, 2) AS gib FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.snapshots GROUP BY ice_table ORDER BY gib DESC",
    "tables": ["snapshots"],
    "tags": ["size", "storage"],
    "cost_hint": "cheap",
    "verified": true
  },
  {
    "kind": "join",
    "slug": "tables-snapshots-manifest-lists",
    "title": "Current manifest set for a table: tables -> snapshots -> manifest_lists",
    "body": "The live manifest set of a table is the manifest-list owned by its current snapshot: `manifest_lists.list_snapshot_id = tables.current_snapshot_id AND manifest_lists.ice_table = tables.ice_table`. Everything else in manifest_lists is history (snapshot-expiry candidates).",
    "sql": "SELECT ml.* FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.manifest_lists ml INNER JOIN ice_meta___CATALOG__.tables t ON ml.ice_table = t.ice_table AND ml.list_snapshot_id = t.current_snapshot_id",
    "tables": ["tables", "snapshots", "manifest_lists"],
    "tags": ["join", "manifests", "current-state"]
  },
  {
    "kind": "join",
    "slug": "tables-objects-orphan-detection",
    "title": "Orphan data files: objects census minus files referenced by manifests",
    "body": "Physical `data.parquet` objects not referenced by any manifest of the table are orphans (safe cleanup candidates after snapshot expiry). Compare `objects` (kind=''data.parquet'') against `data_files(prefix)` file_path per table — needs one data_files call per prefix (see data-files-no-cross-table-view).",
    "sql": "SELECT o.path FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.objects o LEFT JOIN ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix = {prefix:String}) df ON endsWith(df.file_path, o.file) WHERE o.kind = ''data.parquet'' AND o.ice_table = replaceAll({prefix:String},''/'',''.'') AND df.file_path = ''''",
    "tables": ["objects", "tables"],
    "tags": ["join", "orphans", "cleanup"]
  }
]
', '__CATALOG__', {catalog:String}), '''', ''''''),
  ''');') AS seed_sql
FORMAT TSVRaw
queries.description: Optional step 4 - only on servers with the chmem memory store (an `mcp` database with entries_raw). Input: catalog. Emits an INSERT that saves ~10 facts (overview, naming/current-state/cost notes, 2 recipes, 2 join paths) describing the ice_meta_<catalog> views, so MCP agents discover them via search_schema/inspect_table/get_memory. The facts payload is also a valid save_memory body. Re-seeding the same slugs needs supersede:true - edit the emitted JSON if you are updating.

queries.name: Generate: per-catalog drill-down Library
queries.sql:
WITH
  throwIf(NOT match({catalog:String}, '^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$'), 'catalog must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ (it becomes a database/role name suffix)') AS _g1
SELECT replaceAll('{
  "format": "altinity-sql-browser/saved-queries",
  "version": 1,
  "exportedAt": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "queries": [
    {
      "id": "iced-__CATALOG__-0",
      "name": "About: __CATALOG__ per-table drill-down",
      "sql": "",
      "favorite": true,
      "description": "Generated for catalog __CATALOG__ by the Iceberg install Library (i5).",
      "panel": {
        "cfg": {
          "type": "text",
          "content": "## __CATALOG__ — per-table drill-down\\n\\nThese tiles use the parameterized views `ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix)` and `parquet_stats(prefix)`, which need a table **prefix** in slash form (`namespace/table`).\\n\\nFill the shared **prefix** filter above — valid values come from:\\n\\n```sql\\nSELECT s3_prefix FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.tables\\n```\\n\\nThe Parquet footer stats entry is deliberately **not** on the dashboard (it reads every data-file footer — slow); run it from the Library when needed."
        }
      },
      "view": "panel"
    },
    {
      "id": "iced-__CATALOG__-1",
      "name": "Data files of a table (__CATALOG__)",
      "sql": "SELECT file_path, file_format, record_count, file_size, partition\\nFROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix = {prefix:String})\\nORDER BY file_size DESC",
      "favorite": true,
      "description": "Every data file of one Iceberg table with rows/bytes/partition. prefix = tables.s3_prefix (slash form, e.g. namespace/table).",
      "panel": { "cfg": { "type": "table" } },
      "view": "panel"
    },
    {
      "id": "iced-__CATALOG__-2",
      "name": "Small-file buckets (__CATALOG__)",
      "sql": "SELECT\\n  multiIf(file_size < 1048576, ''a: <1 MiB'',\\n          file_size < 16777216, ''b: 1-16 MiB'',\\n          file_size < 134217728, ''c: 16-128 MiB'',\\n          ''d: >=128 MiB'') AS size_bucket,\\n  count() AS files,\\n  round(sum(file_size) / 1048576, 1) AS total_mib\\nFROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix = {prefix:String})\\nGROUP BY size_bucket\\nORDER BY size_bucket",
      "favorite": true,
      "description": "Small-file problem detector: data files of the table bucketed by size. Many files under 16 MiB = compaction candidate.",
      "panel": { "cfg": { "type": "bar", "x": 0, "y": [1], "series": null } },
      "view": "panel"
    },
    {
      "id": "iced-__CATALOG__-3",
      "name": "Partition skew (__CATALOG__)",
      "sql": "SELECT partition, sum(record_count) AS rows, count() AS files\\nFROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.data_files(prefix = {prefix:String})\\nGROUP BY partition\\nORDER BY rows DESC\\nLIMIT 30",
      "favorite": true,
      "description": "Rows and file count per partition value of the table - a lopsided chart means partition skew.",
      "panel": { "cfg": { "type": "hbar", "x": 0, "y": [1], "series": null } },
      "view": "panel"
    },
    {
      "id": "iced-__CATALOG__-4",
      "name": "Parquet footer stats (__CATALOG__) - SLOW, on demand",
      "sql": "SELECT * FROM ice_meta___CATALOG__.parquet_stats(prefix = {prefix:String})",
      "favorite": false,
      "description": "Compression ratio, row-group counts from every Parquet footer of the table. Reads each file footer (~4s / 40 files) - run on demand, never a live tile.",
      "panel": { "cfg": { "type": "table" } },
      "view": "table"
    }
  ]
}
', '__CATALOG__', {catalog:String}) AS library_json
FORMAT TSVRaw
queries.description: Step 5, once per catalog. Input: catalog (must already be installed). Emits a complete saved-queries Library JSON with the per-table drill-down tiles (data files, small-file buckets, partition skew, on-demand Parquet footer stats) bound to ice_meta_<catalog> - database names cannot be query parameters, so these tiles are generated per catalog. Copy the raw result, save it as iceberg-<catalog>-drilldown.json, then File > Append it. The tiles share one `prefix` filter (values = SELECT s3_prefix FROM ice_meta_<catalog>.tables).
